payload.summary: Discovery completed for the health-routing surface. Current code does not gate readiness on database availability, so traffic can still be routed while the DB is down.

facts.claim: Repository scripts define only `build` and `test`; there is no `typecheck` script.

references.detail: `scripts`: `build: "tsc -p tsconfig.json"`, `test: "npm run build && node --test dist/test/*.test.js"`; no `typecheck` entry exists.

facts.claim: The non-interactive build command is `tsc -p tsconfig.json`, which performs a TypeScript compile under strict settings.

references.detail: Compiler uses strict mode (`"strict": true`) and outputs to `dist`.

facts.claim: The non-interactive test command is `npm run build && node --test dist/test/*.test.js`.

references.detail: `test` script chains compile and Node test runner over `dist/test/*.test.js`.

facts.claim: `HealthService` currently ignores database availability in all probe methods; readiness is based only on process state.

references.detail: `liveness` returns `this.processState.running`; `readiness` returns `this.processState.running`; `startup` returns `this.processState.running && this.processState.started`; none call `database.isAvailable()`.

facts.claim: The `DatabaseHealth` interface exposes `isAvailable(): Promise<boolean>`, but `HealthService` does not use it anywhere, so DB outages are not represented in probe decisions.

references.detail: Interface defines `isAvailable()`; implementation in tests provides `MutableDatabase.isAvailable`, but production methods in `HealthService` never call it.

facts.claim: Kubernetes readiness routing exists in deployment config (`/health/ready`), while startup/liveness probes point to their own endpoints; readiness is the primary gate used to route traffic to ready pods.
Current tests do not validate DB-health behavior for readiness/startup/liveness.

references.detail: Tests construct `HealthService` with `new MutableDatabase()` always true; assertions only cover process state transitions and do not assert DB availability impacts.

commands.name: npm test
commands.purpose: Non-interactive test entrypoint currently used in this repository.

testGaps: No test covers `readiness` returning false when `DatabaseHealth.isAvailable()` is false.
No test ensures startup/liveness/readiness behavior changes under DB unavailability.
No repository test exercises HTTP routing/infrastructure probe handling (`/health/ready`) beyond service method contracts.
constraints: Must use strict TypeScript (`tsconfig.json` has `"strict": true` and related strict flags).
Do not run deploy/apply/external infrastructure commands.
Keep deployment parameters unrelated to health routing unchanged.
No `typecheck` npm script exists; only `build` and `test` scripts are defined.
Read-only discovery only; no file writes or command execution beyond inspection in this turn.
assumptions: Readiness endpoint `/health/ready` is the control used by platform to stop routing traffic; this aligns with standard Kubernetes behavior and the provided `deployment.json` probe definitions.
unknowns: Whether deployment wiring actually maps readiness probe failures to external load balancer traffic behavior is not verifiable from these files alone.
Whether additional service/router code outside this repository further gates traffic on DB health is not present in the inspected surface.
